DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFF RONALD RIVERS' MOTION TO COMPEL DOJ: THE MOTION IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. THE PROOF OF SERVICE FOR THE MOTION ONLY SHOWS IT WAS SERVED ON THE DEPARTMENT OF JUSTICE AND DOES NOT SHOW IT WAS SERVED ON DEFENDANTS. THE PROOF OF SERVICE FOR THE REPLY DOES NOT SHOW WHO IT WAS SERVED UPON. THE SUBPOENA FORM THAT WAS SERVED IS FOR A TRIAL OR HEARING. IT WAS NOT APPROPRIATE FOR A DEPOSITION SUBPOENA. THE COURT IS NOT RULING ONE WAY OR THE OTHER ON WHETHER THE MOTION WOULD HAVE BEEN GRANTED IF IT HAD BEEN PROPERLY SERVED AND THE SUBPOENA HAD BEEN ON THE PROPER FORM. THE COURT WILL PREPARE AN ORDER. JUDGE PRO TEM: BRUCE HIGHMAN, CLERK: S. LE, NOT REPORTED. =(302/JPT)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-27-2023 Continued To Mar-13-24 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT ; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities, Declaration
Off Calendar Dec-21-2023 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: Appearances via Zoom Conference
Ronald Rivers, 510-940-5764, Plaintiff in Pro Per
MICHAEL J. VARTAIN, Esq., VARTAIN LAW GROUP, P.C. 415-391-1155 mike@vartainlaw.com Attorneys for Defendants STEPHENS INSTITUTE dba ACADEMY OF ART UNIVERSITY, et al,

Matter is not argued. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF RONALD RIVERS MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

Plaintiff's motion for summary judgment is off calendar. The motion violates the court's order staying this case. The motion is also procedurally defective. Plaintiff seeks summary judgment on his complaint, but does not present evidence to support such an order. The parties resolved this dispute through arbitration.

Order is signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/RBU)

Notice Of Hearing On Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award
Off Calendar Dec-21-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom Conference
Ronald Rivers, 510-940-5764, Plaintiff in Pro Per
MICHAEL J. VARTAIN, Esq., VARTAIN LAW GROUP, P.C. 415-391-1155 mike@vartainlaw.com Attorneys for Defendants STEPHENS INSTITUTE dba ACADEMY OF ART UNIVERSITY, et al,

Hearing is held. ARGUED. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT ELISA STEPHENS, SUE ROWLEY, REID RAUKAR, MARGUERITE CROOKS, MICHAEL VARTAIN, STEPHENS INSTITUTE Notice Of Hearing On Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award

Defendants' petition to confirm the arbitration award is granted. Plaintiff fails to establish one of the limited, enumerated grounds to vacate or correct the award. (CCP 1286.2; CCP 1286.6.) The court already determined that the parties had an enforceable arbitration agreement and there is no basis to revisit that ruling.

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/RBU)

Off Calendar Dec-20-2021 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF RONALD RIVERS' Motion To Compel Compliance To An Issued Subpoena Duces Tecum On San Francisco Police Department is Off calendar at the request of the moving party.

Judge Pro Tem: David McDonald, Clerk: S. Le, Not reported. =(302/JPT)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Doj
Off Calendar Dec-16-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Ronald Rivers, (510.940.5764; rivers_ronald@ymail.com) Plaintiff in propria persona.
Brian Segal, Esq. (916.210.3356; brian.segal@doj.ca.gov) for non-party, CA DOJ

THE PARTIES STIPULATE TO THE MATTER BEING DECIDED BY THE JUDGE PRO TEM. THE PARTIES AND THE JUDGE PRO TEM APPEARING REMOTELY AUTHORIZE THE CLERK TO SIGN THE FORMAL STIPULATION FORM ON THEIR BEHALF.

ARGUED. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF RONALD RIVERS' Motion To Compel DOJ:

The motion is denied without prejudice. The proof of service for the motion only shows it was served on the Department of Justice and does not show it was served on defendants. The proof of service for the reply does not show who it was served upon. The subpoena form that was served is for a trial or hearing. It was not appropriate for a deposition subpoena. The Court is not ruling one way or the other on whether the motion would have been granted if it had been properly served and the subpoena had been on the proper form.

The Court will prepare an order.

Judge Pro Tem: Bruce Highman, Clerk: S. Le, Not reported. =(302/JPT)
